import heapq

class MedianFinder:

    def __init__(self):
        """
        initialize your data structure here.
        """
        # max_heap stores the smaller half of numbers.
        # We use a min-heap by storing negative values to simulate a max-heap.
        self.max_heap = [] 
        
        # min_heap stores the larger half of numbers.
        self.min_heap = []

    def addNum(self, num: int) -> None:
        # By default, add the new number to the max_heap (smaller half).
        # Store as -num to maintain max-heap property.
        heapq.heappush(self.max_heap, -num)

        # Balance condition 1: Ensure all elements in max_heap are <= all elements in min_heap.
        # If the largest element in max_heap is greater than the smallest in min_heap,
        # it means an element is in the wrong heap. Move it from max_heap to min_heap.
        if self.max_heap and self.min_heap and (-self.max_heap[0] > self.min_heap[0]):
            val = -heapq.heappop(self.max_heap) # Pop from max_heap (get actual value)
            heapq.heappush(self.min_heap, val) # Push to min_heap
        
        # Balance condition 2: Maintain heap sizes.
        # max_heap should either have the same number of elements as min_heap,
        # or exactly one more element (when total numbers are odd).
        
        # If max_heap becomes too large (more than one element larger than min_heap),
        # move its top element to min_heap.
        if len(self.max_heap) > len(self.min_heap) + 1:
            val = -heapq.heappop(self.max_heap) # Pop from max_heap
            heapq.heappush(self.min_heap, val) # Push to min_heap
        
        # If min_heap becomes larger than max_heap,
        # move its top element to max_heap.
        if len(self.min_heap) > len(self.max_heap):
            val = heapq.heappop(self.min_heap) # Pop from min_heap
            heapq.heappush(self.max_heap, -val) # Push to max_heap (as negative)

    def findMedian(self) -> float:
        if len(self.max_heap) == len(self.min_heap):
            # If the total number of elements is even,
            # the median is the average of the largest element in max_heap
            # and the smallest element in min_heap.
            return (-self.max_heap[0] + self.min_heap[0]) / 2.0
        else:
            # If the total number of elements is odd,
            # the median is the largest element in max_heap.
            return float(-self.max_heap[0])
